args: Could you please get my ai-leadership agent to look into two things: SHould I use perplexity for research, or it therre a case to use it for everything? With the speed of new tools in the AI space, how do I balance using these tools deeply and getting them set up, vs ensuring I am using the best/most cost effective tool.

seed.intent: Could you please get my ai-leadership agent to look into two things: SHould I use perplexity for research, or it therre a case to use it for everything? With the speed of new tools in the AI space, how do I balance using these tools deeply and getting them set up, vs ensuring I am using the best/most cost effective tool.

args: I would like to launch two new projects. The first projectIs building on the PAI infrastructure. I want a better to-do listThis to-do list should allow me to put things on my to-do list, not all of which are going to be done today. I should be able to allocate these tasks to a particular project or area of my life. I should be able to prioritize them. I should be able to pull them into what I'm gonna do today and put that into kind of a schedule. For example, I'm gonna do items A, B, and C from 9 to 11, I'm gonna do D E F from 1 to 3. The rest I'm gonna leave till tomorrow. Now I can have that as a running to do list which I can update easily.There should be a nice UI for me to be able to add things and move things around on my to-do list, for example, move them between different areas of my life, add note to it a note to a particular item, re-prioritize items, de deprioritise items, put them in a backlog of sorts. So we are talking a bit like a ticketing system. Maybe we want to build it on top of GitHub in some way. That's one thing to consider.I'm currently still using my notebook because nothing comes close to think and that just write it down and have it there.I would also like to have an agent that runs against this to do list and asks me for updates. Did I get this task done? Is that task still relevant? Do I need to make changes? So there should be an agent, essentially a personal assistant, who is w can constantly reviewing this to-do list they might point out gaps they might point out things I've forgotten that is something else I want as part of this engineThe second one is a completely different projectEssentially what I want to do is I want to be able to do a zero-based budget using envelope budgeting integrating into my bank feedsAt the moment I would use what Dave Ramsey and his team have, but their app doesn't connect to Australian banks. What I currently use is PocketSmith, but it doesn't do the whole zero-based budgeting, envelope budgeting version. Very well So at the moment my primary bank account is with ANZ. I also have my main credit card is with NAB. I also have an account with Macquarie. My thinking is that I could actually just use PocketSmith, which currently already integrates into all these banks. Bank feeds to the get the transactions out. That would include a categoryI should then be able to assign these categories to a particular envelope budget. So there may be parent categories and subcategories all feeding into the same envelope budget.I should be able to set a date at a past time and see what my envelopes looked like then and I should also see what be able to see obviously what the envelopes look like nowThe envelopes should be run on a cash basis For each envelope I should be able to see both what the envelope total is but also how I'm looking at the budget for that month. So a particular budget envelope might have money put into it each month or it might have an annual budget that should always be prorated down to monthly and I should be able to see how I'm progressing against that within the month, within the year, etc. etc. There should be an easy UI for me to use with this it should be both web and mobile friendlyThere should be an option to sync my PocketSmith. We're gonna have to work out the best way to do that. I'm not sure if PocketSmith allow APIs or if I'm going to have to do an export with PocketSmith and then import it into to this app but something to consider is could we do that with an interceptor? And finally the other thing to consider is how can we feed this budgeting system, which is my view of what my budgeting should look like. into the PAI pulse dashboard. Please consider these two projects. The first step is to get a great understanding of what we want to build. Clarify the requirements with me. Clarify how if I want it to work. These should be run as two completely separate projects. Ideally you're going to spin up a separate thread for each one and they will appear separately in my cloud code agents display Within each of those threads, then obviously there w

args: Here is the list of things I'd like to achieve for my career pivot today. I would like to act on the referral I got for to Virgin Australia for a senior AI engineer. So I need to put that application together. Then I want to do some research on what consulting jobs are available at companies like Bain news strategy and accenture strategy and I want to do some research if I was to broaden my location and be open to say Sydney, Brisbane or even London, what would the opportunities look like and are there opportunities I should consider? Replying to feed my career pivot. The next thing I'd like to achieve today is I want to apply for two product manager roles in Melbourne. I want to try preference roles that are looking for someone with a software engineering background, ideally a smaller company that would value that cross skill set. Then I would like to apply for two software engineering jobs. These should be senior software engineering jobs in Melbourne that have a strong AI focus at a really good company. I need to find out which ones I want to do. I can probably feed off my seek saved list. List but I should also see what else is out there to identify what the best two jobs to apply for today are. The next thing on my list is to do some outreach. I want to reach out to Max Antonov to see if I can get on his list for mentoring. I would then also like to complete my ADP list profile and book in a session with new Nuna Alles, Daniel Karzell, Hong Thang Vo, and I also want to search for one more to really try and interrogate if there's someone better I could be looking at there. These are what I want to achieve for my career pivot today. So there are a few distinct streams The first is the version application. In addition to that which I haven't mentioned yet, I also need to get back to a recruiter on LinkedIn regarding a senior product engineer job The second stream is the consulting companies. The third stream is the product. And the fourth stream is the software engineering. The fifth stream is the outreach. Please spin up agents so that these will all appear as threads in my claude  agent view

args:
Stream 1 — Virgin AU application + LinkedIn recruiter reply

  You are picking up Stream 1 of Nick's career-pivot push from a prior thread that has been closed. Self-contained context follows.

  ORIGINAL ASK (Nick's words):
  "I would like to act on the referral I got for Virgin Australia for a senior AI engineer. So I need to put that application together. I also need to get back to a recruiter on LinkedIn regarding a senior product engineer job."

  WHAT THE PRIOR AGENT PRODUCED:
  A. Virgin Australia
  - Role pinned: "Senior Engineer, AI" (NOT "Senior AI Engineer"), Brisbane QLD / Sydney NSW listing, AI Centre of Excellence inside Data & Analytics / IT. URL:
  https://careers.virginaustralia.com/jobs/senior-engineer-ai-brisbane-qld-australia-sydney-nsw
  - JD keywords: production AI/LLM/RAG/agentic; LLMOps/AgentOps eval+red-team+observability; multi-agent + MCP; Python/FastAPI primary; AWS (AgentCore/Strands nice-to-have); tech leadership; cross-functional comms; responsible AI
  guardrails.
  - Cover letter drafted (referral-led, [Referrer name] placeholder, leans on ANZ Plus Vertex AI + Gemini production work, AccessBuddy live, LIMSOC SLSA national strategy).
  - 6 tailored CV emphasis bullets drafted for SWE variant.
  - Critical gap flagged: role lists Brisbane/Sydney only, NO Melbourne. Needs referrer confirmation on Melbourne-hybrid availability, separate unlisted Melbourne req, or Nick's relocation intent.
  - Material gap: JD leads with Python/FastAPI; Nick's production AI work is Golang. Recommend one honest sentence acknowledging this rather than overclaiming.

  B. LinkedIn recruiter — senior product engineer
  - Draft reply template ready: peer-tone thanks + openness + asks for comp band, on-site/hybrid, AI focus, team size, runway + short value-prop line.

  NICK'S ANSWERS TO OPEN QUESTIONS (he will fill in below before sending you this — if any are blank, ask him directly):
  - Virgin Melbourne/relocation status from referrer: ___
  - Referrer's name (to fill the [Referrer name] placeholder): ___
  - Python framing call — honest one-line acknowledgement OK? ___
  - LinkedIn recruiter name: ___
  - LinkedIn recruiter company: ___
  - LinkedIn recruiter role title/link: ___
  - Paste of the recruiter's original message: ___

  WHAT TO DELIVER:
  1. Finalised Virgin cover letter with referrer name + location/Python lines resolved.
  2. Final SWE-variant CV emphasis bullets ready to slot into his resume markdown.
  3. Finalised LinkedIn recruiter reply mirroring the original message specifics.
  4. Surface anything still blocking submission.

  CONTEXT FILES TO READ:
  - /home/nicholas-wood/.claude/PAI/USER/RESUME.md
  - /home/nicholas-wood/.claude/PAI/USER/PRINCIPAL_IDENTITY.md
  - /home/nicholas-wood/Documents/workspace/career/ISA.md
  - /home/nicholas-wood/Documents/workspace/career/agents/positioning/memory.md (note: prior agent flagged a stale Heidi TODO on lines 24-25 that needs replacing — content provided in prior thread if Nick approves the rewrite)

  CONSTRAINTS:
  - Australian English, no em dashes, executive-ready tone.
  - Drafts only — Nick reviews and submits everything himself. No outbound actions.
  - career-positioning agent: analysis/drafting only, no committing artefact files.
  - Return under 800 words.

args:
Stream 3 — PM roles Melbourne (2 to apply)

  You are picking up Stream 3 of Nick's career-pivot push from a prior thread that has been closed. Self-contained context follows.

  ORIGINAL ASK (Nick's words):
  "Apply for two product manager roles in Melbourne. Preference roles that are looking for someone with a software engineering background, ideally a smaller company that would value that cross skill set."

  WHAT THE PRIOR AGENT PRODUCED:
  Honest finding: Melbourne PM market is thin for this filter. Only 2 strong fits surfaced — agent did not pad.

  Pick #1: FrankieOne — Senior PM, KYC (Melbourne, hybrid, ~A$155-181K est). 9/10 fit.
  - JD explicitly names "engineering background or strong technical depth" as desirable, spec-driven dev workflow, Claude Code use.
  - ~90-person Airtree Series A. KYC/AML/fraud orchestration API.
  - URL: https://careers.frankieone.com/jobs/7470683-senior-product-manager-kyc
  - CAVEAT: aggregators show 27 May deadline but role still live on FrankieOne careers + LinkedIn — apply today and email hiring team direct.
  - Cover letter draft: leans on AccessBuddy end-to-end + ANZ/Macquarie compliance-grade work + SLSA C-suite consulting engagement.

  Pick #2: Heidi Health — PM, Intelligence (Syd or Melb, hybrid 3 days). 8/10 fit.
  - Wants LLM concept fluency, builder-PM not process-PM. Clinical data → AI-native insight surfaces.
  - $100M raised, Melbourne-founded, equity-backed.
  - URL: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/heidihealth.com.au/50d0963c-c178-42c6-b28a-815cf2e3f1ef
  - STRATEGIC: Nick has existing warm contacts from prior Heidi Tranche 2 outreach (Mo Ismail, Thamasi Perera, Nell Hardie). Warm path > cold portal.
  - Cover letter draft: frames LSV operational comms platform as the same shape as Heidi Intelligence (raw operational data → decision-changing surfaces). Honest about clinical-domain newness.

  Rejected/closed (do not revisit): Octopus Deploy PM, Heidi PM Models, Heidi PM Apps, RecordPoint (wrong domain), Lorikeet (no PM open), Relevance AI (no PM live), HUG (opaque shell).

  NICK'S ANSWERS TO OPEN QUESTIONS:
  - FrankieOne: submit-and-email-direct, or any FrankieOne contacts to route through first? ___
  - Heidi: warm path via Mo/Thamasi/Nell before cold portal — yes? ___
  - Comp floor (both silent on salary): ___
  - Heidi Melb vs Syd office split — verified Melbourne availability? ___

  WHAT TO DELIVER:
  1. Finalised FrankieOne cover letter + 4 CV emphasis bullets.
  2. Finalised Heidi cover letter + 4 CV emphasis bullets.
  3. If Nick chose Heidi warm path: draft the brief warm note to Mo/Thamasi/Nell.
  4. If FrankieOne contact named: tailor the direct-email opener.

  CONTEXT FILES TO READ:
  - /home/nicholas-wood/.claude/PAI/USER/RESUME.md
  - /home/nicholas-wood/.claude/PAI/USER/PRINCIPAL_IDENTITY.md
  - /home/nicholas-wood/Documents/workspace/career/ISA.md (for Heidi Tranche 2 contact details)

  CONSTRAINTS:
  - Australian English, no em dashes, executive-ready tone.
  - Drafts only — no submissions.
  - Don't invent achievements. Reword existing ones.
  - Return under 1000 words.

args:
Stream 4 — Senior SE AI roles Melbourne (2)

  You are picking up Stream 4 of Nick's career-pivot push from a prior thread that has been closed. Self-contained context follows.

  ORIGINAL ASK (Nick's words):
  "Apply for two senior software engineering jobs in Melbourne that have a strong AI focus at a really good company. I need to find out which ones I want to do. I can feed off my Seek saved list. But I should also see what else is out there
   to identify the best two jobs to apply for today."

  WHAT THE PRIOR AGENT PRODUCED:
  Honest finding: narrow pool for "senior + applied AI in product surface + great company + Melbourne." Two picks with conviction.

  Pick #1: Airwallex — Senior Software Engineer, AI Tooling (Melb or Syd). A$160-220K + equity + bonus.
  - Internal AI security/automation tooling, applied LLMs in production.
  - Melbourne-founded $5B+ fintech, strong eng culture.
  - URL: https://careers.airwallex.com/job/63aeda84-dcf8-4ad0-be04-2771c269ca35/senior-software-engineer-ai-tools/
  - Caveat: sits inside InfoSec — thin wrapper over platform/infra/AI work, but worth Nick's call.
  - Cover letter draft: "operator's mindset applied to applied AI" — ANZ observability + AccessBuddy production ownership + LIMSOC SLSA.

  Pick #2: Xero — Engineer, AI Platform (remote-AU, Melbourne-eligible). A$160-199K est.
  - LLM gateway, agentic platform, eval/observability tooling. AWS + Terraform + Kubernetes + Python.
  - URL: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/xero/42672767-6055-4ac2-b499-3b34a9a4448f
  - TITLE FLAG: posting is "Engineer" not "Senior" — agent's read (and mine) is that the JD is senior-equivalent based on scope and comp band. Apply with SWE CV.
  - Cover letter draft: leans hard on Nick's PAI infrastructure as unstated-on-resume proof point.

  Rejected: Zendesk Staff (comp ceiling lower, step-up too far), Lorikeet (Sydney only), Relevance AI (no Melb req), Eucalyptus (Sydney, role stale), Canva (already applied + rejected — CAR-3), REA (AI framing vague).

  NICK'S ANSWERS TO OPEN QUESTIONS:
  - Seek saved list (paste URLs/titles — agent could not access his Seek account): ___
  - Comp floor (both clear A$160K — confirm if higher): ___
  - Companies to exclude (recent rejections, conflicts, recruiter overlap): ___
  - Airwallex InfoSec context — green light or too narrow? ___
  - Xero "Engineer" not "Senior" title — apply anyway? ___

  WHAT TO DELIVER:
  1. If Seek saved list provided: re-rank picks against the saved list — replace either pick if a stronger one surfaces.
  2. Finalised Airwallex cover letter + 4 CV emphasis bullets.
  3. Finalised Xero cover letter + 4 CV emphasis bullets, addressing the title discrepancy in the letter.
  4. Updated needs-from-Nick if anything still blocking submission.

  CONTEXT FILES TO READ:
  - /home/nicholas-wood/.claude/PAI/USER/RESUME.md
  - /home/nicholas-wood/.claude/PAI/USER/PRINCIPAL_IDENTITY.md
  - /home/nicholas-wood/Documents/workspace/career/ISA.md (CAR decision log for exclusions)

  CONSTRAINTS:
  - Australian English, no em dashes.
  - Drafts only — no submissions.
  - Be opinionated. If Seek saved list reveals a fit that beats Airwallex or Xero, say so and re-rank.
  - Return under 1000 words.

  WHAT THE PRIOR AGENT PRODUCED:
  Honest finding: narrow pool for "senior + applied AI in product surface + great company + Melbourne." Two picks with conviction.

  Pick #1: Airwallex — Senior Software Engineer, AI Tooling (Melb or Syd). A$160-220K + equity + bonus.
  - Internal AI security/automation tooling, applied LLMs in production.
  - Melbourne-founded $5B+ fintech, strong eng culture.
  - URL: https://careers.airwallex.com/job/63aeda84-dcf8-4ad0-be04-2771c269ca35/senior-software-engineer-ai-tools/
  - Caveat: sits inside InfoSec — thin wrapper over platform/infra/AI work, but worth Nick's call.
  - Cover letter draft: "operator's mindset applied to applied AI" — ANZ observability + AccessBuddy production ownership + LIMSOC SLSA.

  Pick #2: Xero — Engineer, AI Platform (remote-AU, Melbourne-eligible). A$160-199K est.
  - LLM gateway, agentic platform, eval/observability tooling. AWS + Terraform + Kubernetes + Python.
  - URL: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/xero/42672767-6055-4ac2-b499-3b34a9a4448f
  - TITLE FLAG: posting is "Engineer" not "Senior" — agent's read (and mine) is that the JD is senior-equivalent based on scope and comp band. Apply with SWE CV.
  - Cover letter draft: leans hard on Nick's PAI infrastructure as unstated-on-resume proof point.

  Rejected: Zendesk Staff (comp ceiling lower, step-up too far), Lorikeet (Sydney only), Relevance AI (no Melb req), Eucalyptus (Sydney, role stale), Canva (already applied + rejected — CAR-3), REA (AI framing vague).

  NICK'S ANSWERS TO OPEN QUESTIONS:
  - Seek saved list (paste URLs/titles — agent could not access his Seek account): ___
  - Comp floor (both clear A$160K — confirm if higher): ___
  - Companies to exclude (recent rejections, conflicts, recruiter overlap): ___
  - Airwallex InfoSec context — green light or too narrow? ___
  - Xero "Engineer" not "Senior" title — apply anyway? ___

  WHAT TO DELIVER:
  1. If Seek saved list provided: re-rank picks against the saved list — replace either pick if a stronger one surfaces.
  2. Finalised Airwallex cover letter + 4 CV emphasis bullets.
  3. Finalised Xero cover letter + 4 CV emphasis bullets, addressing the title discrepancy in the letter.
  4. Updated needs-from-Nick if anything still blocking submission.

  CONTEXT FILES TO READ:
  - /home/nicholas-wood/.claude/PAI/USER/RESUME.md
  - /home/nicholas-wood/.claude/PAI/USER/PRINCIPAL_IDENTITY.md
  - /home/nicholas-wood/Documents/workspace/career/ISA.md (CAR decision log for exclusions)

  CONSTRAINTS:
  - Australian English, no em dashes.
  - Drafts only — no submissions.
  - Be opinionated. If Seek saved list reveals a fit that beats Airwallex or Xero, say so and re-rank.
  - Return under 1000 words.

args:
Stream 5 — Outreach: Max Antonov + ADPList

  You are picking up Stream 5 of Nick's career-pivot push from a prior thread that has been closed. Self-contained context follows.

  ORIGINAL ASK (Nick's words):
  "Reach out to Max Antonov to see if I can get on his list for mentoring. Complete my ADPList profile and book in a session with Nuna Alles, Daniel Karzell, Hong Thang Vo, and search for one more to really try and interrogate if there's
  someone better I could be looking at there."

  WHAT THE PRIOR AGENT PRODUCED:
  A. Max Antonov pinned: Product Director at HealthShare NSW (NSW Health). Prior: THE ICONIC, HOYTS, News Corp AU. 15+ yrs product/eng. Runs fractional coaching at antonov.com.au, 3+ yrs Startmate mentor. Two coaches at a time, currently
  full — outreach is a waitlist play.
  - CHANNEL: Email max@antonov.com.au (his site explicitly directs to email, do NOT LinkedIn-DM first).
  - Outreach email drafted: peer-tone, specific to his cross-sector arc, names the pivot narrative as the gap.

  B. ADPList profile:
  - Section checklist + bio (~150 words, journey-led) + goals statement drafted.

  C. Three booking notes drafted, each names a specific outcome (not generic career advice):
  - Nuno Alves (Product Lead, Canva): first-12-months in PM coming from eng at a product-mature company. adplist.org/mentors/nuno-alves
  - Daniel Karzel (SWE+PM dual role at 10101): how to make informal SWE+PM legible externally. adplist.org/mentors/daniel-karzel
  - Hong Thanh Vo (Senior PM, Dayforce): enterprise PM path from technical background. ADPList profile not publicly indexed — channel may need to be LinkedIn DM (au.linkedin.com/in/vohongthanh).

  NAME SPELLING FLAG: Nick's brief gave "Nuna Alles", "Daniel Karzell", "Hong Thang Vo". Closest confirmed profiles are Nuno Alves, Daniel Karzel, Hong Thanh Vo. CONFIRM before booking.

  D. Fourth mentor recommended with conviction: Venkatesh Arunachalam, MD Impekable Australia, Fractional CPO. ThoughtWorks → VMware Tanzu Labs → fractional CPO. Booking note positioned around pivot-narrative stress-test for Accenture
  Strategy / Nous / tier-one consulting hiring managers.

  NICK'S ANSWERS TO OPEN QUESTIONS:
  - Confirm three name spellings (Nuno Alves / Daniel Karzel / Hong Thanh Vo correct?): ___
  - Existing ADPList account or starting fresh? ___
  - Hong Thanh channel: ADPList if account exists, else LinkedIn DM? ___
  - Any session theme to swap (e.g. Nuno for AI-in-product instead of transition path)? ___
  - Max waitlist worth pursuing, or move on given full status? ___

  WHAT TO DELIVER:
  1. Finalised Max email (or replacement direct-DM if Nick deprioritises waitlist).
  2. Finalised ADPList bio + goals (ready to paste).
  3. Three finalised booking notes (with confirmed channels).
  4. Finalised Venkatesh booking note.
  5. Surface anything still blocking outreach.

  CONTEXT FILES TO READ:
  - /home/nicholas-wood/.claude/PAI/USER/PRINCIPAL_IDENTITY.md
  - /home/nicholas-wood/.claude/PAI/USER/TELOS/PRINCIPAL_TELOS.md
  - /home/nicholas-wood/.claude/PAI/USER/RESUME.md
  - /home/nicholas-wood/.claude/PAI/MEMORY/RESEARCH/perplexity/2026-06-10__*.md (Hong Thanh ADPList source check)

  CONSTRAINTS:
  - Australian English, no em dashes, peer voice (not deferential).
  - Drafts only — Nick sends everything himself.
  - Specific outcomes per session, not generic mentor messaging.
  - Return under 1000 words.

args:
Prompt for Project 1 — Inkwell thread (unchanged from previous)

  Open a dedicated thread for project: PAI Task System (working name "Inkwell").

  This thread owns this project end-to-end. You are the principal architect, orchestrator, and delegator for Inkwell. You do not build code yourself; you decide what to build, you maintain the ISA, and you delegate implementation to
  feature-dev and QA subagents.

  # Read these files first (in order)

  1. ~/.claude/PAI/MEMORY/WORK/pai-task-system/REQUIREMENTS_CLARIFICATION.md
  2. ~/.claude/PAI/MEMORY/WORK/pai-task-system/ARCHITECT_REVIEW.md (the staff-architect critique; cuts question set from 42 to 13, commits to "Inkwell", three architecture flips, 14-day notebook bake-off as done condition)
  3. ~/.claude/PAI/DOCUMENTATION/IsaFormat.md
  4. ~/.claude/PAI/USER/PRINCIPAL_IDENTITY.md and ~/.claude/PAI/USER/PROJECTS/PROJECTS.md

  # What to do first

  The architect review identifies 5 absolute-minimum questions Nick must answer before any building:

  - L1: Primary capture device — phone or laptop?
  - L3: Canonical store — SQLite (architect's recommendation), markdown, or Postgres?
  - L4: Agent autonomy — propose-and-confirm or autonomous?
  - L8: Bake-off success metric — accept the proposed 80%/90% or set your own?
  - A4: Relationship to the Algorithm's ISA/ISC system — parallel, subordinate, or subsumed?

  Open by surfacing these five questions with the trade-offs and ask Nick to answer. Then ask the other eight (L2, L5, L6, L7, A1, A2, A3, A5) only if his answers leave them genuinely undecided. Once answered, write the project ISA at
  ~/Documents/workspace/inkwell/ISA.md (per Algorithm v6.0+ doctrine: project ISAs live at the project root). Then delegate the MVP build (CLI + SQLite + Telegram capture + 17:00 daily check-in) to a feature-dev subagent.

  # Operating mode

  PAI conventions. bun + TypeScript. No Python. No npm/npx. No em dashes. Australian English. Use Algorithm mode for anything multi-step. Treat Nick as a peer — strong opinions loosely held, pushback welcome.

  This is a Life OS project, not just a todo app. Build the smallest thing that beats his paper notebook in a two-week bake-off.

args:
Prompt for Project 2 — Penny / EnvelopeOS thread (refreshed — review is now done)

  Open a dedicated thread for project: Penny (codebase folder: envelope-os). Zero-based envelope budgeting layer on top of PocketSmith, feeding PAI Pulse. AUD. Single user. Personal-finance-grade security.

  This thread owns this project end-to-end. You are the principal architect, orchestrator, and delegator. You do not build code yourself; you decide what to build, you maintain the ISA, and you delegate implementation to feature-dev and QA
  subagents.

  # Read these files first (in order)

  1. ~/.claude/PAI/MEMORY/WORK/envelope-os/REQUIREMENTS_CLARIFICATION.md (full clarification; sections 5.4 and 6.8 patched with verified PocketSmith API findings)
  2. ~/.claude/PAI/MEMORY/WORK/envelope-os/ARCHITECT_REVIEW.md (staff-architect critique; PocketSmith API is free + ungated + Developer Key + OpenAPI 3 spec; flips Postgres → SQLite, Next.js → Vite SPA; cuts MVP to four weekend phases;
  names "Penny"; defines 7 load-bearing questions plus 3 new ones)
  3. ~/.claude/PAI/DOCUMENTATION/IsaFormat.md
  4. ~/.claude/PAI/USER/PRINCIPAL_IDENTITY.md, ~/.claude/PAI/USER/PROJECTS/PROJECTS.md, ~/.claude/PAI/DOCUMENTATION/Pulse/PulseSystem.md

  # What to do first

  The architect review identifies 10 micro-decisions Nick must lock before the ISA is written. Surface them in this order with the architect's recommendations:

  LOAD-1: Period = calendar month, every-dollar-allocated as hard UI rule. Confirm.
  LOAD-2: Credit-card model = A (spend-date) with a VISIBLE Credit Card Holding envelope. Confirm.
  LOAD-3: Default rollover = roll positive + zero negative; negatives carry as visible debt with no auto-true-up. Confirm.
  LOAD-4: Mapping = many-categories-to-one-envelope; splits OUT of MVP. Confirm.
  LOAD-5: Unmapped categories land in "Inbox" envelope. Confirm.
  LOAD-6: UI = envelope grid (Dave Ramsey); allocation = beginning-of-month wizard with "apply last month" default. Confirm.
  LOAD-7: Pulse widget = three tiles: unallocated $, top 3 overspend %, Holding balance vs NAB statement. Confirm or swap (c).
  NEW-1: Reconciliation cadence for Holding envelope = end-of-statement-period click. Confirm or specify.
  NEW-2: Variable income handling = expected-vs-actual line on dashboard. Confirm (you're potentially going independent).
  NEW-3: Bake an unused `owner` column into the data model for forward-look joint finances. Confirm.

  Plus: SQLite vs Postgres (architect strongly recommends SQLite); Penny vs Envelopes for working name.

  Once locked, write the project ISA at ~/Documents/workspace/envelope-os/ISA.md at E3 tier with the full twelve-section body. Include the codegen-from-OpenAPI decision in `## Constraints`. Then delegate Weekend 1 build (PocketSmith API
  client codegen + SQLite schema + daily sync + backfill script) to a feature-dev subagent.

  # Operating mode

  PAI conventions. bun + TypeScript. No Python. No npm/npx. No em dashes. Australian English. AUD. Use Algorithm mode for anything multi-step. Treat Nick as a peer — strong opinions loosely held, pushback welcome.

  Local-first by default. SQLite over Postgres. Tailscale-only mobile access. PocketSmith developer key in PAI secrets store. No cloud SaaS storage of financial data.
